raw_output:
PR #5 correctness review (Codex CLI 0.145.0, gpt-5.6-sol, read-only sandbox, lane-B range at dd565f3).

Findings: X1 (P2) engineering-standards.md presents security-hygiene/links/lint-type as enforcement despite the wave plan recording only review-gate and tests as required checks — the new jobs run but do not prevent merge. X2 (P2) documented tag-based release convention has no corresponding tag history (no local or origin tags, including for the recorded v0.1.0); consumers cannot use the advertised stability mechanism. X3 (P3) "every PR passes the battery" (README, ADR 0003) contradicts the documented intentionally-unattested probe PR #2; qualify as every merged PR. X4 (P3) ADR 0004 and 0005 omit Considered Options, below even MADR's minimal form.

Overall: documentation mostly well cross-checked against gate, CI configuration, coverage settings, history, artifacts, and lane-B touch-set. Main correctness gap: several CI standards presented as binding before their checks are required; the release-tag claim is inoperative.

--- Post-review delta note (orchestrator, attested at final head 83d6f55) ---
The four legs reviewed the lane-B range at dd565f3 (stacked on unmerged lane
A). Single round, zero blocking findings. What changed after the legs ran:
(1) fix batch 9cc8a41/1a1e99a closing every validated finding — docs
rewritten from intended posture to shipped state (tags marked nonexistent
pending a release decision; advisory jobs labeled advisory; "no secrets"
corrected to the ephemeral read-only token; gate-job hermeticity narrowed to
the stdlib-only script + SHA-pinned first-party actions; Socket attributed to
its GitHub App install; escalation rule labeled procedural), Phase 5
equivalence claim replaced with an explicit wider-window concession + a
committed-report-matches-delivered-message requirement, delta notes given a
definition in the process doc, mutation-check precedent re-attributed to
PR #1, Considered Options added to ADRs 0004/0005, wave-1 report blocking
count clarified; (2) rebase onto main after PR #4 merged (lane-B content
unchanged; range diff now equals the main...HEAD diff this hash covers);
(3) the wave-2 report committed per the Phase 5 option-C convention this PR
itself introduces — the report matches the message delivered to Kristov.
Verification on the final head: markdown-only diff (no code paths), all
relative links resolve, git diff --check clean, full test suite still 58
passed with the 99 floor. Why no re-run: every change is finding-responsive
doc correction or the process records the convention prescribes; no code,
gate, CI, or instruction-semantics change beyond the reviewed Phase 5 text
whose final wording implements the Claude security leg's own S1 remedy.

findings.summary: New quality jobs described as enforcement while not required checks
findings.reason: Standards table labels all three advisory pending required-check promotion — escalated as wave-2 report decision 1.

findings.summary: Tag convention documented with no tag history
findings.reason: Same fix as Claude F2/S3: docs state no tags exist; first cut escalated (wave-2 report decision 2).

findings.summary: 'Every PR passes the battery' contradicts the recorded probe PR #2
findings.reason: Qualified to every merged PR in README and THREAT_MODEL; probes noted as hard-blocked by design.

findings.summary: ADRs 0004/0005 omit Considered Options (below MADR minimal form)
findings.reason: Considered Options sections added to both.
